name: claude-command-bridge
description: "Use when the user mentions a Claude slash command or command recipe that exists under .claude/commands/*.md, for example `/build-fix`, `/cancel-ralph`, `/checkpoint`, `/clean_gone`, `/code-review`, `/commit-push-pr`, `/commit`, `/create-plugin`, `/e2e`, `/feature-dev`, `/hookify-configure`, `/hookify-help`. Read the matching .claude/commands/<name>.md file and follow it as a Codex-adapted workflow guide."
---

<!-- generated by scripts/sync_claude_to_codex.py -->

# Claude Command Bridge

1. If the user mentions `/name`, strip the leading slash and check `.claude/commands/name.md`.
2. Read only the matching command file first.
3. Treat that file as a workflow guide, not as literal Claude-only syntax.
4. Translate unsupported Claude-specific concepts such as hooks, slash commands, `Task`, or `AskUserQuestion` into native Codex behavior.
5. If the command file dispatches specialized agents, load the matching repo skills under `.codex/skills/`.
6. If no matching command file exists, report that briefly and continue with normal Codex behavior.
